guests.name: Hurrikan Press
guests.description:
Hurrikan Press is a small press and publishing house, founded in 2014, located in the 8th district of Budapest, Hungary. We are mostly working with risograph machines, currently old RPs and GRs, with 14 colors.

The press is run by Árpád Szigeti and Zsófia Rumi, both illustrators and printmakers. Our main goal is to print our own work and to publish zines by local and international folks that focus on street art, alt comics and drawing.

guests.name: Hilda kolektiv
guests.description: Hilda (*2022) is a collective of artists formed out of friendship, whose practice is based on the principles of collaborative work and polyphonic creation. Their collaborative work is defined by an emphasis on process, mutual influence, and respect for the diverse perspectives and experiences of each member. The collective does not strive for a unified aesthetic, but rather for a layered interconnection of approaches that take shape through dialogue and collaborative situations. A fundamental role in their work is played by the effort to articulate contemporary experience through materials, forms, and images that carry memory and affective charge. Inspiration from the figure of Hildegard of Bingen frames their interest in synthetic modes of cognition that interconnect spiritual, visual, and environmental dimensions. The collective actively participates in exhibition and creative projects and undertakes joint artist residencies. Its members explore and experiment with what it means to be and create together – what forms collaborative work, shared responsibility, and the constant reevaluation of the collective’s very functioning can take.

guests.name: Kěř press
guests.description: Publishing grafted literature & seeding chaos. Kěř Press is a newly founded DIY publishing house; we organize Bookjam workshops and print zines and graphics. We’re interested in experiments and playing with words, text, and images. We love creating together, typesetting, and remixing. Slowly nurtured by Jakub Klimeš and Pája Smékalová. Ústí nad Labem and Prague.

guests.name: tomáš staněk a nora třísková
guests.description:
Urban stilllifes, wilderness and landscape in photography (nora) and illustration (tomáš), pragensie and all sorts of bushes and křáks. we have several zines, riso and digitally printed, some risographics and posters, one or two authors books.

@tms.stnk1 @nora.tri

guests.name: XAO
guests.description: Zaozaz, Čaočač, Maomam, Kaokak, Laolal, Daodad, Uaouau, and more we could have been, but in the end we are Xaoxax. We are a group of illustrators who run a tiny gallery, non-commercial mini-bookstore and also mikro-publishing house. We respect pigeons, mushrooms and flies.

guests.name: projekt Moribundus
guests.description: We are a riso studio and micro-publishing house based in Wallachia. We primarily print photography zines, activist non-periodicals, and prints of all kinds. We strive to support unknown and underappreciated artists and to demonstrate that riso printing and photography go hand in hand. We avoid pristine finishes and branded papers.

guests.name: Risko print
guests.description:
Risko Group/Collective:
We’ve been printing art, humor, and politics on an old RP3700 press for 6 years now. We’re part of the KUBIK community center in Trnava. We take on commissions and create for fun – graphics, posters, zines, stickers, notebooks, and whatever else comes to mind. Sometimes we draw something, sometimes we print something.

guests.name: Kudla Press
guests.description:
Kudla Werkstatt is an independent risograph studio and publishing house that strives to support nonprofit organizations and serves as an open platform for collaboration with artists from various fields. The emphasis is on quality and the overall execution of the work.
To date, Kudla Press has published more than 40 original publications featuring primarily visual art, illustration, or comics, as well as hundreds of small and large prints and other delightful items :)

guests.name: FŮD
guests.description: Fůd magazine was founded in 2015 by three illustrators (Veronika Homolová, Bára Müllerová, and Barbora Tögel) as an independent magazine, which later evolved into a platform for socially engaged illustration called FŮD. We’ve already published 8 issues and organized several exhibitions, both in the Czech Republic and abroad. Over the years, we’ve featured works by illustration and comic superstars such as Tara Booth, Daisuke Ichiba, Ida Neverdahl, and many others. We always bring together artists with diverse styles, both well-known and lesser-known, from the Czech Republic and abroad. This year we’re celebrating our 10th anniversary and bringing you juicy magazines, zines, and posters.

guests.name: Veronika Homolová
guests.description:
Veronika Homolová is an illustrator and a punch needle enthusiast.
She is interested in the human body, mythology, and cosmogony. And since she doesn’t know how to draw people, she personifies everything else. To Zinek she’ll be bringing her bestseller Love Story, an original comic story in which Heart and Brain fall in love. And also a few new pieces. All riso prints and all with faces.

Anna Kulíčková is an illustrator from Prague. She focuses primarily on book illustration and independent art projects. She draws inspiration from everyday situations and life in general. At Zinek, you would be able to purchase one of her indigo prints inspired by her dog František.
guests.name: Anna Kulíčková
Bára Müllerová
guests.description: Bára Müllerová is an illustrator, herb collector, and backpacker. She draws comics and herbal calendars, and enjoys paper cutting. In addition to commissioned work, she creates her own zines, tinkers with risograph printers, and fills her space with paper scraps.

guests.name: Dark Press
guests.description: DarkPress is a risograph printing studio and independent publishing house that has been in operation since 2020. We collaborate with illustrators, painters, and photographers on their projects, helping them bring their work to print. Together with partner platforms and artists, we organize exhibitions and produce our own publications. As fans of backpacking and darker music, our publishing house focuses on releasing zines related to these themes, and under the Osten label, we release small-run recordings by local bands. Our prints and publications are regularly featured at domestic and international markets and festivals.
